subject: Research Qwen3-ASR-0.6B CPU inference path + streaming support
learnings: Official qwen-asr Python streams only via vLLM (GPU); antirez/qwen-asr C engine is the real CPU path — but its interactive --stream re-encodes the partial tail and falls 2.3x behind real-time paced input.

subject: Scaffold TuParles project (git init, poetry, package layout)
Spike: backend selection on Fr-En code-switched audio
learnings: Whisper-family on CPU costs a fixed ~30s encoder window per call regardless of audio length; faster-whisper chunk_length is a no-op on encoder compute. Always lspci before CPU latency engineering — an idle RTX 4080 invalidated a day of careful CPU arithmetic in one benchmark.

subject: Build bilingual spoken-punctuation post-processor
learnings: Bare 'point' must map for French dictation but is everyday English vocabulary — shield by context (determiner/compound before, 'of' after), since dictated punctuation arrives as a standalone clause-final word.

subject: Build audio capture module (16 kHz mono ring buffer, level meter)
learnings: 33ms blocks (SAMPLE_RATE//30) give 30fps RMS levels for free in the same callback that accumulates audio.

subject: Build text delivery (xdotool type into focus + clipboard backup)
learnings: xdotool type at 12ms/key reads as a UI freeze on long transcripts; also never self-test type-into-focus from a background agent — it lands in whatever window the user has open.

subject: Build streaming transcription engine with partial results
learnings: Greedy beam=1 partials, condition_on_previous_text=False so a mishear can't snowball across calls; final re-decodes with full beam.

subject: Build global hotkey daemon (RCtrl+RAlt)
learnings: pynput observes without suppressing — global keys still reach focused apps; toggle+hold-PTT+Esc all ride one passive listener.

subject: Build floating bubble UI (waveform + live transcript)
learnings: Frameless translucent always-on-top + WA_ShowWithoutActivating so it never steals focus from the dictation target.

subject: Build transcript history (local store + search CLI)
learnings: SQLite in XDG data home survives repo moves; every call opens a fresh connection (the cross-restart story).

subject: Build notification-area tray menu
learnings: DBus/AppIndicator: aboutToShow is unreliable AND rebuilding a shown menu invalidates its layout (flash-close). Build eager, refresh change-aware.

subject: Fix keyboard lock during post-recording typing
learnings: xdotool --clearmodifiers re-presses modifiers after typing (xdotool#43) → stuck AltGr feels like a locked keyboard; explicit keyup instead.

subject: Add view setting: minimal vs full
learnings: Full view grows to MAX_HEIGHT then head-ellipsis trims the oldest words (later memoized + bisected for the paint loop).

subject: Generate README screenshots from code
learnings: QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen renders the real widgets — no doctored mockups, regenerable after any UI change.

subject: Open-source pre-flight: license, scrub, public
learnings: filter-repo mailmap scrubs identity, but repo-local user.email MUST be the noreply or every new commit re-leaks the work email.

subject: Desktop integration: .desktop entry + icon
learnings: Desktop entry needs the resolved venv path; gtk-launch from a shell loses stdout — launch via systemd-run for journald.

subject: GitHub Actions CI: lint + tests
learnings: CI runs pure layers only (no GPU/Qt) — 'CI can't hear you scream'; the daemon/ui/hotkey are validated by hand.

subject: Releases: tagged versions + curl install.sh
learnings: Tag-triggered gh release with install.sh as the downloadable asset; force-pushing a rewritten tag needs explicit refs/tags/ target.

subject: Language settings: searchable multi-toggle list
learnings: detect-then-snap forced ONE language per take — which BREAKS code-switching; superseded by multilingual=True (#33-era insight).

subject: CONTRIBUTING.md + dev tooling formalized
learnings: Two-tier testing doctrine: pure layers in CI, human-in-the-loop for audio/Qt/X.

subject: Vocab tooling: suggest/review/add from history
learnings: Glossary stays suggestions-only (auto-grow = autocorrect); initial_prompt hot-reloaded per decode; greedy partials echo the prompt so it's dropped there.

subject: Hold-to-talk mode
learnings: A hold must never stop a take it didn't start — press_started_take guards against a stray combo-release ending an ongoing toggled take.

subject: Collapse Whisper repetition loops
learnings: Collapse on sentence units, preserving separators + newlines, casefold compare; runs of >=3 identical → one.

subject: Bubble placement: cursor screen, sticky desktops
learnings: xprop _NET_WM_DESKTOP 0xFFFFFFFF = sticky on all virtual desktops; QApplication.screenAt(QCursor.pos()) picks the cursor's screen.

subject: Fix post-stop freeze: batched decode + paste
learnings: BatchedInferencePipeline VAD-parallel: 66s→1.3s; paste over 200 chars. But the real minute-long freeze was downstream delivery (see #29).

subject: Local telemetry: per-take metadata + stats
learnings: Schema migrates via PRAGMA table_info + ALTER; faster-whisper exposes avg_logprob + word probs natively (perplexity is one field away).

subject: Hide glossary prompt-echo from live preview
learnings: Greedy partial decode echoes initial_prompt verbatim ('Glossaire:…') on short audio → drop the prompt on partials, keep it on the final.

subject: Investigate post-stop freeze (Sicily take 2)
learnings: Three misattributions (GPU recompute, paint loop, busy receiving app) — each a real smaller bug. Root cause: non-ASCII xdotool keymap-remap MappingNotify storm + paste-then-type fallback. Read journal + history DB before theorizing.

subject: Profile stop→paste pipeline per step
learnings: Per-step journal breakdown proved decode ~1s and deliver was the cost; the killer was xdotool ctrl+v timing out → re-typing. Instrument every step, don't guess.

subject: Portable audio backend: mic selection + Bluetooth/headset-aware
learnings: Store the chosen mic by NAME, not PortAudio index — plug in a Bluetooth/USB device and every index after it shifts, but names are stable. Re-resolve at each take; if the name is gone (headset disconnected), rescan once then fall back to default rather than kill the take. Guard `import sounddevice` (try/except OSError,ImportError → None) so the pure device-matching logic imports without libportaudio — that's what lets it run in CI. PortAudio snapshots its device list at init, so a hotplugged mic needs sd._terminate()/_initialize() to appear.

subject: Voice command meta-language: delete/undo/nudge by voice, no cloud
learnings: Command-vs-dictation is the whole problem; solve it STRUCTURALLY, not with a confidence score. The doubled trigger ('efface efface') is the interlock — nobody doubles a verb in prose, so a single trigger is always text. Bias is asymmetric: a false positive edits the user's prose (unforgivable), a false negative just types a command they retry (trivial) — so when in doubt, it's text. Literal-escape must be CONDITIONAL (fire only when the remainder would itself parse as a command) or it hijacks every sentence starting with 'dis'/'say'. The adversarial prose corpus IS the spec — write it first, the grammar is what's left once misfires are impossible. Reused the paste key-senders (xdotool/ydotool) for edits with the same never-raise contract. Pure-voice layer needs no hotkey changes and is fully unit-testable without a GPU — that's why it shipped before the 'lead design' held-modifier quasimode (#50). Sentence-unit deletion was dropped: no keystroke deletes 'a sentence' reliably across apps, and an unreliable edit is worse than an honest refusal.

subject: Adversarial code-switch eval suite: corpus + multi-engine WAV gen + Whisper integration test
learnings: TTS franglais is the realistic adversarial signal — a FRENCH voice reading English tokens reproduces the exact phonetics that trips Whisper (the seed: 'fan out'->'fais un air'). Multi-engine (piper+espeak) all ffmpeg-normalised to 16k mono so the harness loads WAVs into the SAME int16 array the mic path makes. Manifest scanned from disk, not from this-run's voices, so regenerating one engine never silently drops another's WAVs. Scoring = slots-gate (must_contain/must_not_contain, contiguous-token match) + WER-trend (never a gate). The corpus is itself under test (no dup ids; every must_contain present in its own reference; every must_not_contain absent).

subject: Add project CLAUDE.md (docs-honesty duty + moat doctrines)
learnings: Project-local CLAUDE.md captures the doctrines where future sessions actually see them: 'it's a setting' (smart default + total override), structural-not-statistical safety, wrong-autocorrect>visible-mishear, measure-before-trust (the eval), MCP-lean. The docs-honesty duty explicitly counts in-product help (cheat-sheet/wizard/tutorial) as documentation — 'a spoken command nobody can discover does not exist.'

subject: Spoken-syntax core: registry + interlocks + settings scaffold
learnings: Interlocks live IN each feature (quotes pair, caps mark next word), NOT in the core — resisted a speculative universal-interlock abstraction (YAGNI; extract when a 2nd family reveals the shared shape). Core only provides: ordered settings-gated registry, a never-crash-the-take contract (best-effort per feature), and a SyntaxContext that threads the output-format target (#58) it does not itself decide. Empty registry = identity, so it wired into pipeline.postprocess safely before any family existed.

subject: Spoken quotes: first syntax family (bilingual, paired, configurable marks)
learnings: The bare-'guillemets' collision (it's also an ordinary word) is solved STRUCTURALLY with a pair-count guard: a lone bare trigger stays text, >=2 makes it quotes. This honors the user's 'bare auto-alternate' choice AND the when-in-doubt-it's-text doctrine simultaneously. Sentinel tokens (\x00qo/qc<lang>\x00) let one hug+substitute pass serve straight/curly/guillemets uniformly, and carry language to mark-resolution. Default marks straight " both langs (tech-friendly) over the user's literal FR-straight/EN-context answer — flagged for correction.

subject: Dict-seed: folder parser-into-dict (symbol extraction)
learnings: The SrcType->weight table (dep 10, def 6, H1 5, ident 3, comment 1) IS the moat and lives in one place (nlp/parse.py). git ls-files gives free .gitignore respect; the kind filter is essential (AlgoliaSaaS: 66% of tracked files are generated fixtures/logs). Python+Markdown get real ASTs; other langs a coarse identifier sweep (tree-sitter deferred, YAGNI). NON-OBVIOUS: raw Sigma-salience is VOLUME-biased -- a dep (weight 10) vanishes under the mass of weight-2 C++ idents (code_ident = 72.9% of total salience, dep = 0.0%).

subject: Dict-seed: TF-IDF ranker over a folder corpus
learnings: Rented sklearn (DictVectorizer + TfidfTransformer) instead of hand-rolling. KEY: norm=None, else a term alone in a doc peaks at 1.0 (l2 artefact) and distinctiveness inverts. The payoff finding: symbol-top15 vs tfidf-top15 overlap = 0/15 -- symbol surfaces const/std/the, TF-IDF surfaces GeoRecord/idxCompiler. TF-IDF is what rescues symbol's volume bias; neither signal is usable alone at scale.

subject: Dict-seed: local-embeddings term signal
learnings: fastembed (ONNX, CPU, no torch) is the lean shippable backend; sentence-transformers wired but deferred (its torch won't import on the CUDA-wedged box). GOTCHA: 'intfloat/multilingual-e5-small' isn't in fastembed 0.8's list -> use 'sentence-transformers/paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2'. Embedding rank is near-ORTHOGONAL to symbol/tfidf (Spearman <=0.08) = genuinely independent signal. And clustering QUARANTINES noise for free: test/assert macros collapse into one cluster -> cluster-based denoising beats a hand stoplist.

subject: Dict-seed: RRF fusion ranker
learnings: RRF is the boring/calibration-free choice, and it's empirically justified: the 3 signals are near-independent (|Pearson|<=0.15, Spearman embed<->others <=0.08), which is exactly when fusion adds value. dictseed combines them as seed_score = RRF(symbol,tfidf[,embed]) * (0.25 + 0.75*whisper_risk) -- prominence GATED by how hard the token is for Whisper, so a salient common French word (risk~0) doesn't get seeded.

subject: EPIC: Local analytics & introspection — telemetry + feature-discovery + Analytics dashboard
learnings: Telemetry framed as introspection, NOT analytics: single-user + local-only dissolves the whole consent/transport layer that makes telemetry fraught. The question that keeps it 'light' and honest is 'which features earn their place / never get discovered?' -- feeds deletion-beats-addition.

subject: Telemetry: telemetry/ package -- primitives + local sink
learnings: event(name, /, **attrs) -- the event-name MUST be positional-only or a name= attr collides (TypeError). Sibling events table added to the existing history.db via CREATE IF NOT EXISTS (additive, history rows untouched); exposed history.db_path() as single source of truth so telemetry reuses the store without coupling on a private.

subject: Telemetry: instrument commands / syntax / entry-path
learnings: syntax.used flows through a pure on_fire hook threaded daemon -> postprocess -> apply_syntax, so the SHARED eval path never gains a side effect. The entry-path indirection (toggle_from_hotkey/_tray) was invisible to the green suite -- needed an offscreen-Qt stubbed-Controller test to verify the source attr. command.fired can't be unit-tested (executor fires real keystrokes).

subject: Telemetry: Réglages kill-switch (local-only opt-out)
learnings: Local-only telemetry => default-ON opt-out is honest (nothing is ever transmitted). The 'effacer mes statistiques' wipe gets a confirmation (data deletion always double-checks).

subject: Telemetry: nlp-over-introspection adapter
learnings: Reused the nlp message_documents path over history.texts(); guarded every nlp-backed call behind nlp_available() so --without-nlp degrades to [] not a crash. usage_summary counts by the feature's own name attr (attr_split), not the coarse event name.

subject: Telemetry: Analytics dashboard (tray -> corpus + utterances + telemetry)
learnings: The corpus view RENDERS cached EDA JSON from disk -- never builds a 50k-term corpus on the GUI thread (the stall watchdog would scream) and sidesteps #70 entirely. Cheap views stay synchronous, no worker thread in v1. HTML builders are pure functions -> testable without a QApplication (matches the grandfathered-UI test boundary). The advisor caught that the green suite hid the untested entry-path wiring.

subject: PII P1: deterministic redaction core (secrets + denylist + frequency-floor)
learnings: Three detectors, ONE safety semantic split: checksum-validated structured PII (stdnum IBAN/NIR/Luhn) and known secret prefixes carry BLOCK authority; high-entropy tokens and topic hits are ALERT-only -- precision earns the right to redact. Denylist matching runs on a normalize() pass (NFKD accent-strip + casefold + leet-map) but only to DECIDE redaction, never on delivered text, so it can be lossy + Scunthorpe-safe (whole-token match). The _CANDIDATE regex must be contiguous (\b[0-9A-Za-z]{10,34}\b) -- an early version allowed spaces and gobbled across tokens. Core is standalone: NOT yet wired into the live utterance/analytics/dict paths (that integration is the next stroke).

subject: PII P1.5: wire deterministic core into live utterance / analytics / dict paths
learnings: The gate before defaulting persist-redaction ON was EMPIRICAL, not a judgment call: does any re-deliver path (repeat/redo/nudge) paste text read back from history.db? Traced it -- the only history.last()/recent() consumers are copy-last (clipboard), the tray history menu, and analytics; NONE re-paste via deliver(). So redacting at persist makes those show minimized text BY DESIGN, not a regression. Verbatim is delivered upstream; only the stored copy is shaped. Settings-aware glue (privacy_policy.py) lives one level OUT of the pure privacy/ core -- keeps the core rentable/standalone. Dict-seed leg deferred: #68 isn't built, don't hook into nonexistent code.

subject: PII: sensitive-content eval corpus + harness (FR+EN red-team, leakage rate)
learnings: Two ASYMMETRIC metrics: leakage (planted secret survived -> must be 0) and over-redaction (clean text masked -> also 0 since detectors are deterministic). First run caught a REAL precision bug: bare Luhn passes ~1-in-10 random digit strings, so a 15-digit order number was masked as Amex -- fixed by requiring a real network IIN prefix + issued length, restoring the high precision that earns block authority. TWO scanners agreed: GitHub push-protection BLOCKED the push because a literal sk_live_ test key sat in the corpus -- the firewall's own red-team material is exactly what the firewall stops. Fix: assemble credential-shaped fixtures at load time via concatenation ({{AWS}}/{{GHP}}/{{STRIPE}} markers); never store a real-looking secret in a tracked file. amend HEAD (push was rejected so nothing was on remote) to purge the secret from the commit tree.

subject: PII: Réglages privacy UI (denylist editor, block/alert tiers + anonymity floor)
learnings: Separate PrivacyDialog (not inline in the already-tall SettingsDialog). Pure parse_terms helper (one term/line, de-duped, order-preserving) tested without Qt; an offscreen-Qt round-trip pins that _save() writes EXACTLY the setting keys active_denylist() reads, so the UI can't silently drift from the engine (same untested-indirection trap the advisor flagged on telemetry). Operator profiles / faker / cloud-egress / fail-closed deferred to #105 -- don't build knobs without a consumer.

subject: CI chore: bump actions off Node 20 -- became a full CI resurrection
learnings: main's CI had been RED for many commits before this session (the 'ruff/mypy clean' in memory was LOCAL-only -- always verify CI, not just local). What looked like a one-line Node bump uncovered SIX stacked root causes, each masking the next because a failing step aborts before later ones run: (1) unpinned ruff auto-bumped on the runner -> new UP rules failed untouched files [pin ruff==0.9.10 + mypy==2.1.0 to poetry.lock]; (2) python-stdnum missing from the runner pip line -> privacy tests couldn't collect; (3) ruff format --check is a SEPARATE gate from ruff check -- ran ruff format over the tree; (4) hotkey.py mypy union-attr because evdev (optional Linux dep) is absent on runners -> cast(Any, key.fileobj); (5) fcntl.flock flagged on Windows cells -> mypy platform='linux' (app is Unix-only, type-check the deploy target); (6) PySide6 absent on runners -> pytest.importorskip guards on Qt-touching tests; (7) numpy 3.12 stub uses PEP-695 'type' which a hardcoded python_version=3.11 rejects -> drop the pin, mypy matches each cell. KEY METHOD: reproduce CI locally (mypy --platform win32, version-matched interpreter, PySide6-absent path) instead of blind push-and-wait -- each CI round-trip is ~3min. Bump was checkout@v5 + setup-python@v6 (Node 24; Node 20 force-deprecated June 2026, removed 2026-09-16).

subject: Onboarding: voice/text bug-report -> prefilled GitHub issue URL
learnings: Tokenless by design: a public repo can't ship a usable token, so build issues/new?title=&body= and open it -- no API, no account data sent (the #87 decision). Auto-gather an environment block (version/python/OS/Wayland-vs-X11) so reports arrive actionable. Pure + offline (urlencode + injectable env), tested via parse_qs round-trip. First piece of onboarding epic #55.

subject: Onboarding: update checker (GitHub Releases API)
learnings: Opt-IN (default off): a network call reveals you run the tool, so local-first means the user chooses; `tuparles update` always works manually. Tokenless public Releases endpoint. Version compare is pure (_version_tuple, v-prefix + short tags, weird tag sorts low, unknown current always offers); fetch is INJECTABLE so tests never socket; every failure path returns None so a dead check can't cost a launch. check_if_enabled() gates on the setting and short-circuits BEFORE fetch.

subject: Dict-seed: feed mechanism -- bias half (initial_prompt) shipped; post-correct deferred to #69
learnings: Shipped the BIAS half only (initial_prompt enrichment from cached EDA top_seeds) -- advisory to Whisper, can't corrupt, so safe to ship on-by-default without GPU validation. KEY: Whisper keeps only the LAST ~224 tokens of initial_prompt, so the curated manual glossary must go at the TAIL (auto-seeds ahead of it) or your hand-picked words get truncated. seed_prompt.initial_prompt(manual, seeds, bias_enabled) is fully injectable -> pure tests, no vocab.txt/EDA touched; engine._vocab_prompt delegates. The POST-CORRECT (transcript rewrite) is deferred to #69's FP/FN harness and #69's description updated to own it -- a rewrite must be MEASURED before trusted. Effectiveness of the bias itself also awaits #69 (mechanism shipped, not yet proven to help). A bias-off-but-seeds-passed bug was caught by tests -> gate seeds unconditionally on bias_enabled.

subject: Onboarding: spoken help ('que peux-tu faire')
learnings: A new 'help' voice command (commands.py) + delivery._show_help fires a fire-and-forget notify-send (Popen, never waited -- per the desktop-feedback memory) with cheatsheet.as_text(brief=True); absent notify-send -> toast points at `tuparles cheatsheet`. Structural safety like #41: whitelist of DISTINCTIVE multi-word phrases (FR+EN), never bare 'aide'/'help' (collide with prose), whole-take exact match under MAX_COMMAND_TOKENS. KEY REUSE: cheatsheet.as_text() is the ONE renderer shared by CLI + notification + future panel, so help can't diverge across surfaces. execute_command's 'help' branch flows through the existing daemon _run_command path -- no daemon change needed. The notification IS the spoken-help surface; the richer in-app panel is #83's deferred Qt deliverable.

subject: Quick-chat: phrase-pack format + deterministic trigger->expansion engine
learnings: Pure anchored engine: a trigger fires ONLY on a whole-take fullmatch (never a substring in prose -- the misfire corpus is the load-bearing test), with <name> template slots compiled to non-greedy named groups. Match case-insensitively via re.IGNORECASE on a whitespace-normalized ORIGINAL (not casefolded) so captured slot values keep the user's case; collapse trigger whitespace at parse time so spacing can't break a match. Pack = hand-editable ~/.config/tuparles/phrasepack.json, re-read every take (hot-reload like vocab); malformed/absent -> [] (a broken file never costs a take). SAFE-ON: quickchat_enabled default True but an empty pack is a no-op, so it can't fire until you write a macro. Wired into the daemon BETWEEN command-handling and delivery (expansion delivered + recorded like dictation). Also surfaced in the cheat-sheet (#83) so user macros are discoverable. GOTCHA: zsh does NOT word-split unquoted $VARS -- list files explicitly in shell gates.

subject: Onboarding: cheat-sheet CORE + tuparles cheatsheet (panel deferred)
learnings: DERIVE-not-hardcode is the whole point: cheatsheet.entries() reads the live grammar -- commands.vocabulary() (new public view of the #41 meta-language), punctuation.SPOKEN_TO_SYMBOL, syntax.catalogue() (each family carries its own summary/triggers, co-located with its regex so help can't drift), and the user's quickchat pack (#89). A humanize() renders the punctuation regexes into readable phrases, GUARDED by a test that fails if a pattern grows a construct it can't render. search() is accent+case-insensitive (NFKD strip). #83 stays pending: only the Qt tray/settings PANEL remains (thin render over entries()/search()).

subject: Casing: re-case engine (target styles, conservative preserve default)
learnings: DESCRIPTIVE casing (match the user's style) is the opposite of PRESCRIPTIVE voice-caps #59 (adds caps on command); they compose via the `protect` hook NOT stage order -- advisor caught that running recase last would DOWN-case exactly the word #59 just capitalized, so #59 must register explicit caps as protected spans. Wiring hinged on one fact: eval.py normalize() casefolds (line 38), so casing is invisible to the code-switch scorer -> postprocess is the safe home AND #123's casing eval needs its OWN case-sensitive scorer. Conservative guards are ORTHOGRAPHIC/model-free (URLs/identifiers/digit-bearing/camelCase/ALL-CAPS>=2) so they live in #120; proper-noun NER is #122 via the same `protect` predicate. Doctrine made structural: default `preserve` = pure identity (ships dark); sentence-case treats a terminator behind a closing quote as NOT a boundary (never capitalize on an ambiguous quote-internal period) -- missed cosmetic beats wrong rewrite. Honest gap until #122: `lower` lowercases proper nouns + plural acronyms (isupper() False on 'APIs'), documented as the lowkey aesthetic. Use unicode str.lower/upper/isupper, NEVER an a-z/a-ÿ regex range (drops oe/ae ligatures past U+00FF -- French-tool footgun).

subject: Syntax family: capitalization — region all-caps (paired, require-close)
learnings: Second spoken-syntax family after quotes (syntax_features/caps.py). Structural interlock = REQUIRE-CLOSE: a region fires only as a complete open...close PAIR ('tout en majuscules'...'fin des majuscules' / 'all caps'...'end caps'), a lone open stays text. Deliberately MORE conservative than quotes' auto-close-at-take-end: an unclosed quote adds one mark, but an unclosed caps-open would SHOUT the whole rest of the take -- the wrong-autocorrect side of the asymmetry. Mode-switch close synonym 'en minuscules'/'minuscule(s)'/'lowercase' is safe ONLY because require-close ignores it without a preceding open. Misfire corpus ('je l'ai ecrit en majuscule') is the load-bearing test, written first. Composes with #120 `lower` FOR FREE: an all-caps region reads as an acronym to #120's guard, so explicit caps survive descriptive lowercase (the #59x#120 conflict only bites future next-word SINGLE caps). KEY SCOPING CALL (advisor): non-preserve casing styles are unreachable today (nothing writes casing_style), so the composition is latent not live -> ship #59 standalone, defer the sentinel/reorder channel to #121 when a UI first enables a style. Shipped 1/3 of the task title; next-word + dual-mode engine -> #126 (needs quasimode #62).

subject: Onboarding core: « Comment Tu Parles ? » first-launch/post-update perso card
learnings: PURE view-agnostic core (onboarding.py): returns axes-as-data + preview + apply, so BOTH planned views (sleek Qt carousel AND a no-Qt `tuparles onboarding` text fallback) ride the same core -- graceful degradation made literal, same core/view split as cheat-sheet #83. Becomes the FIRST UI that writes casing_style -> wakes #120 AND flips the #59x#120 composition from latent to live (=> the #121 work). Trigger logic via two settings: onboarding_done (first launch) + onboarding_axes_seen (post-update re-surfaces ONLY axes added since, so a finished user is undisturbed until a release adds one); force=True = manual replay. preview(key,value) runs the REAL engine (casing.recase) so the animated card can't show a style the pipeline wouldn't produce -- honesty over flash. HONEST GAP: 'Ton role' records the choice (role setting) for #90 to consume later; its preview names the role, never fakes a macro. Four axes: casing(#120)/role(#90)/languages(list-parsed)/view. Elicited scope with the user (AskUserQuestion): all 4 axes, auto-cycling carousel + live-input demo, all 3 triggers. Design note: docs/research/2026-06-24-comment-tu-parles-onboarding.md.

subject: Bubble UX & engine-status pass + breathing tray (SPEC.md → v0.2.0)
learnings: Six SPEC papercuts from the first real XPS22 run (KDE Plasma 6 Wayland, RTX 2060). PERCEPTUAL MIC METER: old linear rms/8000 over full int16 left quiet/mid speech near-flat; gate(NOISE_FLOOR)+speech-scale(FULL_SCALE)+gamma(0.6) makes soft speech visibly move bars. Key insight: the bars JUMPING is itself the 'recording started / I hear you' cue, so §2 also answered the user's separate worry about a start indicator. ENGINE COLOUR (green=GPU/blue=CPU): wired as a backend_source PULL closure mirroring the existing level_source, NOT a per-take push signal; ResilientEngine.active_backend flips to 'cpu' only on the sticky session fallback, so the colour is honest about which silicon is live. Applied to bubble bars AND tray glyph. final=brighter/whiter green flash (distinct from GPU-green), error=red. PROCESSING SWEEP: a travelling exp-pulse across bars, distinct from idle breathing and the final flash. DEFAULT VIEW flipped minimal→full. WAYLAND POSITIONING: chose XWayland (QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb set in daemon.run before QApplication) over LayerShellQt — cheapest cross-machine fix so move()/xprop work; degrade-honest if forced native wayland (_make_sticky no-ops when platformName()!='xcb' + a startup note, no pretending). BREATHING TRAY: 10Hz QTimer animates a 3-bar glyph (calm breath+bob at rest, livelier undulation recording, travelling pulse decoding). Real cost is DBus icon churn on SNI trays (GNOME/KDE) not CPU → gated by tray_animation setting (default on), applied LIVE via SettingsDialog.accepted→Tray.apply_live_settings (no restart). MULTI-MONITOR: bubble_screen setting (pin-to-named-screen default 'primary', or 'cursor' to follow mouse); vanished pin degrades to primary. DEFERRED: mirror-on-all (touches the Wayland paste-hide focus path) and follow-focused-window (focus-screen not reliably queryable on native Wayland — would ship a setting that silently doesn't work). OS notifications (notify-send for help) are compositor-placed; not app-controllable. Tests: perceptual mapping, active_backend transitions, screen targeting+picker; all Qt tests use pytest.importorskip('PySide6') so they SKIP on no-Qt CI.

subject: CI rescued (format + Windows UTF-8) and first minor release v0.2.0
learnings: CI (ci.yml) has FOUR gates on a {ubuntu,macos,windows}×{3.11,3.12} matrix: ruff check, ruff format --check (SEPARATE from check — passing lint ≠ passing format; I twice pushed red here), mypy, pytest. WINDOWS matrix caught a pre-existing class of bug: Path.read_text()/write_text() without encoding= uses the locale default (cp1252 on Windows) → UnicodeDecodeError (a ValueError) on UTF-8 files. test_example_pack_is_valid failed because the example phrasepack loaded empty (caught→[]); CHANGELOG/vocab.txt with accents would have too (whatsnew's read was even uncaught). Fixed all 11 sites with encoding='utf-8' (JSON paths ASCII-safe via json.dumps but made explicit). CONVENTION going forward: always pass encoding='utf-8' to read_text/write_text. RELEASE is tag-driven: release.yml on a pushed v* tag → gh release create $TAG install.sh --generate-notes. Releasing had LAPSED at v0.1.0 (2026-06-11) despite ~11 sprints → cut catch-up v0.2.0 (pre-1.0 semver: features=minor). PUSH-RACE GOTCHA + RECOVERY: a concurrent push from the other machine advanced origin/main mid-tag, so the tag+auto-release got cut from an OFF-MAIN commit missing role-phrasepacks, and main lacked the version bump. Recovery: rebase the bump onto origin/main, push main, `gh release delete v0.2.0 --yes --cleanup-tag`, `git tag -f v0.2.0 HEAD`, push tag → release.yml recreates from the correct commit. Lesson: fetch before tagging; --generate-notes uses the commit list, not the curated CHANGELOG. Push auth: HTTPS fails in this env (no askpass) → origin switched to SSH (BatchMode), PLN works directly on main.

subject: Fix bubble final-flash hue contract (green=GPU only)
learnings: Green meant two things at once — backend identity AND the success flash — colliding exactly on CPU (blue->blue->green). Fix: signal 'landed' via brightness (_brighten lerp toward white), never hue. One visual channel, one meaning. Extracted _bar_color() so the contract is unit-testable headless.

subject: Live partials on the CPU backend
learnings: 'qwen can't stream' was really 'can't with qwen' — faster-whisper is ALREADY a core dep and its CT2 CPU backend needs no CUDA, so a tiny base model on CPU is cheap, not a new heavy dep. The daemon partials loop already self-paces (tail window + elapsed-subtracted cadence), so a slow CPU decode degrades to fewer partials, never a backlog. Partials != final (smaller model) by design.

subject: Recalibrate mic meter to a quiet laptop mic
learnings: Level constants are per-machine. FULL_SCALE=3000/floor=80 (XPS22 desktop mic) left the quiet 4080-laptop mic's normal speech (~80-100 RMS) UNDER the floor -> flat bars. A meter calibrated above the mic's speech floor is indistinguishable from a broken one. Forensics (pavucontrol + RMS probe) before theory.

subject: Capture empty-decode misses (audio + forensic log)
learnings: 'Rien entendu' while partials showed text was a TRIPLE black hole: history.record() returns None on empty text -> no row -> no id-keyed WAV, and the forensic print only reached the launching terminal (the daemon is started from a shell, not journald, when run manually). The failure most worth debugging left zero trace. Fix: takes.save_miss() writes empty-final audio to takes/misses/miss-<ts>.wav (microsecond-stamped so two misses/sec can't collide; own 64MB self-pruning budget) + daemon prints a miss: line (raw decode, last_partial, timings). DOCTRINE: instrumentation bias should be INVERTED -- a clean landed take is self-evident, a miss is a mystery, so it deserves MORE trace not none.

subject: Stamp CPU take language from the partials engine
learnings: qwen runs --silent and emits NO language, so every CPU take had blank lang/lang_prob -- the code-switch signal that is the whole point of the tool went dark on the fallback path. The CpuPartialsEngine (whisper base) DOES detect language and we threw it away. Fix: CpuPartialsEngine keeps last_language/last_language_prob; QwenCpuEngine.transcribe borrows it. Honest about provenance: it's the small partials model's guess, a signal not ground truth; None when partials are off.

subject: replay_takes.py --engine cpu
learnings: The drift harness hardcoded GpuEngine(), so it was useless with the card wedged. --engine cpu runs the vendored qwen binary so real takes replay through the engine that actually served you. qwen takes no initial_prompt, so the CPU path skips the seed regimes entirely (one decode per take). Replay goes through the ENGINE, not the recorder -- so it can reproduce transliteration errors from a WAV but NOT recorder-teardown stalls.

subject: Long-take freeze: move stop()+decode off the GUI thread (#9,#10)
learnings: ROOT CAUSE via the new stop_s forensic: recorder.stop() blocked the GUI thread 65s while the decode was 1s -- an EPISODIC PortAudio teardown stall. Critically NOT length-driven: take 157 (40.7s, LONGER) stopped in 0.03s; take 162 (38.9s) took 65s. That falsified my own snapshot-O(n2) theory with the data table -- DON'T THEORIZE PAST YOUR DATA. Fix: stop()+decode now run on the worker thread (GUI only flips state + spawns), with a _finishing bool guarding all THREE stop entry points (toggle/Esc/combo-release) against a re-entry race. Shipped the sub-timing diagnostic (pa_stop/pa_close/concat, prints only when >0.5s) FIRST and alone, because the stall is episodic + not reproducible from a WAV (replay bypasses the recorder) -- instrument, then wait, then refactor.

subject: Partial recovery belt (#11)
learnings: On a lost final decode (exception OR empty-while-a-partial-was-painted), copy the last partial to the clipboard + toast 'partiel copié (Ctrl+V)' -- NEVER auto-paste a provisional (the final is the truth and this isn't it). Key distinction surfaced by forensics: the screenshot 'freeze' take actually LANDED fine (876 chars in history), so the belt is a net for true crashes, NOT the freeze fix -- told the user plainly rather than letting the belt masquerade as the cure.

subject: Persistent heartbeat watchdog (#10)
learnings: The existing GUI-stall QTimer only logs once the main thread RESUMES, so a whole-system freeze that ends in a reboot kills the process before it ever fires -- useless for the user's actual case. A plain-thread hb: beat every 15s into journald SURVIVES reboots: the last beat dates when we went silent, and gui_lag (now - the GUI thread's last tick) separates a GUI-only hang (beats flow, lag high = OUR bug) from a whole-box freeze (beats stop cold = not us). FREEZE VERDICT: likely NOT TuParles -- Optimus laptop (Intel iGPU drives the display, the 4080 is compute-only, so our CUDA is on a different GPU than the compositor), no OOM in any boot, daemon exited cleanly; the real GPU errors were nvidia-drm 'Failed to grab modeset ownership' = the classic Optimus display-handoff DRIVER bug.

subject: Doubled-comma collapse + ellipsis trois petits points (#6,#7)
learnings: DOUBLED COMMA: saying 'virgule' makes Whisper ALSO transcribe a prosodic comma for the pause -> 'test, ,'. _tidy collapse is COMMA-ONLY and exact (never merges different marks so '?!' survives, never touches the ellipsis glyph or '...' which have no comma) -- doctrine-clean: only removes a provably-redundant comma. ELLIPSIS: 'trois petits points' is far more ambiguous than the formal 'points de suspension' because it's everyday conversational French; solved structurally with a determiner-shield (les/des/ces trois petits points = mention = text, bare = dictation) mirroring the existing English bare-'point' PROTECTED_PATTERNS. Emits the real U+2026; formal triggers switched to it too. When in doubt, it's text.
